title: iris × germanica subsp. albicans
intro: Iris × germanica subsp. albicans, also known as the white German iris or white Dutch iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, characterized by its white flowers and tall, upright foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Iris × germanica subsp. albicans include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Iris × germanica subsp. albicans can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be used both internally and externally to harness its medicinal properties.
